title: Intense Smoky Japanese Tofu Tsukune
description: A plant-based Japanese dish featuring smoky tofu tsukune with a molten, resinous flavor, perfect for brunch, and infused with the sharpness of pecorino-romano cheese.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine crumbled tofu, pecorino romano cheese, smoked paprika, sesame oil, soy sauce, and maple syrup, and mix until well combined.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger to the bowl and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Gradually add breadcrumbs to the dough and mix until it is no longer sticky.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a small ball.
Flatten each ball slightly into a tsukune shape.
Place the tsukune on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the tsukune in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, flipping them halfway through.
While the tsukune are baking, prepare a sauce by whisking together soy sauce, maple syrup, and sesame oil in a small bowl.
Once the tsukune are done, brush them with the prepared sauce and garnish with chopped scallions.
